method: Unity-only isolated material-cell lookdev before full weapon assembly
north_star: steampunk pressure pistol realism: burnished brass, oxidized copper, black iron, ivory gauge, smoked amber glass, chipped red enamel, walnut/leather grip

cells.evidence: Continuous coil silhouette, copper tarnish, brass collars, dark barrel contrast, and small fasteners are all present.
cells.final_product_application: Use as the material/silhouette target for the pressure-pistol coil assembly.

cells.evidence: Dial face has readable tick hierarchy, brass bezel, red needle, smoked glass layer, and rivets.
cells.final_product_application: Promote tick/needle/glass layering into a final gauge prefab before full weapon assembly.

cells.evidence: Receiver plate shows brass variation, polished edge strips, rivets, dark recess, and engraved grime marks.
cells.final_product_application: Use as the base language for receiver side plates and pressure-chamber panels.

cells.evidence: Black iron barrel reads separate from soot, brass bands, blue steel chamber, muzzle ring, and edge scratches.
cells.final_product_application: Use as barrel/muzzle material target, keeping vents and soot readable in first person.

cells.evidence: Red enamel stripe has raised surface, chipped dark underlayer, brass guide lines, and screw scale cues.
cells.final_product_application: Use sparingly as a first-person gameplay readability accent and safety selector material.

cells.evidence: Transparent amber tube, glow core, reflection bands, and brass collars are visible.
cells.final_product_application: Treat as proof for gauge glass, ampoule windows, and small pressure vial inserts.

cells.evidence: Walnut grain and leather wrap direction are usable, but the proxy silhouette remains primitive and needs final sculpt/mesh work.
cells.final_product_application: Use only as material direction and grip proportion guidance, not as final geometry.
